proved: The Rust provider task runtime executed against an in-process literal-loopback fake Responses endpoint using an injected credential.
Rust serialized the actual content-free run record and passed it directly to the TypeScript test process without a hand-authored intermediate fixture.
The serialized record passed the committed Draft 2020-12 schema and the TypeScript cross-field semantic validator.

The record identifies its actual test destination with executionMode=loopback-conformance; semantic validation allows only literal loopback in that mode and continues requiring HTTPS for product remote records.
The serialized record did not contain the credential or prompt canaries.
MCP embeds the updated schema and reports cross-language-certified-unwired rather than product availability.
notProved: Generation is callable from the product UI or Tauri handler.
A real provider endpoint, credential, policy, retention mode, usage, or cost.
Native human disclosure approval and approval lifetime/content binding.
Streaming, tools, reasoning continuity, retries, or adversarial multi-provider orchestration.
